metadata.title: Why a Polished Lesson Plan Can Fail Every Student in the Room
metadata.purpose: Slate cut of Candidate 01. Mechanism: Claude optimizes artifact quality, not the cognitive gap between what students know and what the outcome requires — a polished plan is a proposal for conditions under which learning might happen, not learning itself.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = the outcome / genuine learning / cognitive work; CRIMSON = the artifact gap / surface fluency / missing link; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: NO AI detection tools, NO history of ed-tech failures, NO comparison to other AI tools, NO enumeration of all ways AI can fail. One mechanism only: artifact quality vs. learning quality are orthogonal — polish does not predict transfer.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B13: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. The lesson plan looks perfect. Warm-up. Vocabulary. Three tasks. An exit ticket. In thirty seconds, Claude produced something that took a skilled teacher an hour to write.

card.copy: Why a polished lesson plan can fail every student in the room
card.sub: the artifact is not the learning

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [TITLE] polished plan, no learning

beats.narration_text: Mara teaches ninth-grade biology. Tuesday, she uses the plan on cellular respiration. Students complete every activity. They can recite adenosine triphosphate. But when she asks what a cell would do without ATP — blank looks.
beats.scene_description: A biology classroom, students at desks completing a worksheet; one student's paper has 'ATP = adenosine triphosphate' written neatly but the student stares blankly ahead; editorial newsprint treatment
beats.image_prompt: B02, biology classroom, high school students sitting at desks filling in a worksheet, one student has written 'ATP = adenosine triphosphate' neatly on paper but stares blankly ahead, warm classroom light, editorial documentary style, desaturated, flat
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL] students complete work, blank look on the exit question

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [STILL] students complete work, blank look on the exit question

beats.narration_text: The artifact was coherent. The learning did not happen. And the plan gave no sign that it wouldn't.

production_viz.label: plan quality vs. learning outcome
production_viz.mechanic: A polished lesson-plan document on the left (TEAL-border card with neat bullet points), beside it a student exit-ticket card with a blank answer field in CRIMSON; a serif label 'coherent artifact' under the plan, 'no learning' under the ticket
production_viz.note: establish the gap between the two objects — they look equally valid until labelled

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] polished plan beside the blank exit ticket

beats.narration_text: A polished lesson plan should predict student understanding. Here is the case where it didn't. Why?

card.copy: A polished plan should predict understanding. Here it didn't. Why?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] THE QUESTION on screen

beats.narration_text: Here is how most people think about this. A better lesson plan means better learning. The teacher gets a good plan, uses it faithfully, students learn. The chain seems solid.

production_viz.label: naive assumption: plan quality -> student learning
production_viz.mechanic: Three TEAL blocks appear left to right, connected by arrows: 'BETTER PLAN', 'FAITHFUL USE', 'STUDENT LEARNING'. Each block fades in with its label. The chain looks clean and complete.
production_viz.note: the naive model — looks solid, will be broken

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the naive chain: better plan -> student learning

beats.narration_text: But Claude does not know what your students know. It does not know the misconceptions they carry into Tuesday's class. It cannot know what understanding looks like for this group, at this moment.

production_viz.label: what Claude cannot see
production_viz.mechanic: The TEAL 'BETTER PLAN' block remains. Below it, three CRIMSON chips appear in a column — labeled 'prior knowledge', 'specific misconceptions', 'evidence of understanding' — each with a line crossing it out as a serif label reads 'Claude cannot access this'.
production_viz.note: the gap is about what Claude cannot access, not what it does wrong

beats.narration_text: So Claude does the only thing it can do well. It optimizes for the artifact. The plan reads like expertise. It sounds like a teacher who knows what they are doing. Because it has learned from millions of lesson plans that look exactly like this.
beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] section — Claude optimizes for artifact quality

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] section — Claude optimizes for artifact quality

beats.narration_text: Take Ms. Park. She asks Claude for a lesson on the water cycle. She gets a fifty-minute plan: a diagram activity, vocabulary matching, a fill-in-the-blank formative check. Students complete every task.

production_viz.label: Ms. Park's lesson plan — the water cycle
production_viz.mechanic: A lesson-plan card with three activity slots: 'Diagram activity', 'Vocabulary matching', 'Fill-in-the-blank check'. Each activity has a teal completion tick as a student dot appears beside each. All three ticks fill. Plan looks complete.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Park's plan with all activities ticked

beats.narration_text: Friday: exit ticket. Explain why rain falls as liquid, not vapor. Twenty-two of twenty-eight students leave it blank. The plan covered the topic. The outcome was never named.

production_viz.label: exit ticket result: 22 of 28 blank
production_viz.mechanic: An isotype grid of 28 student squares. 22 turn CRIMSON labeled 'blank'. 6 remain TEAL labeled 'answered'. A serif label below: 'outcome never named in the plan'.
production_viz.note: count from chapter example seed — 22 of 28

beats.narration_text: A lesson plan is a proposal for conditions under which learning might happen. It is not learning itself. That distinction is the hinge the whole problem turns on.

card.copy: A lesson plan is a proposal for conditions under which learning might happen. It is not learning itself.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] quote — proposal vs. learning

beats.narration_text: So what do you actually do? Before you use what Claude gives you, ask one question: what would a student have to be able to DO by the end of this — specifically? Not 'understand the water cycle.' Explain why rain falls as liquid.

production_viz.label: the practice: name the outcome before the plan
production_viz.mechanic: Two column cards side by side. Left (CRIMSON-border): 'Understand the water cycle' — vague. Right (TEAL-border): 'Explain why rain falls as liquid, not vapor' — specific and observable. A serif label appears under the right card: 'name this first'.
production_viz.note: the concrete practice — observable vs. topical

beats.narration_text: When you bring that outcome to Claude, it becomes a backward-design partner. When you skip it, you get coverage without transfer. The artifact looks the same either way. The learning does not.

production_viz.label: fixed chain: outcome first -> aligned plan -> transfer
production_viz.mechanic: The naive three-block chain from B05 returns. A new TEAL block 'NAMED OUTCOME' is inserted at the start, pushing 'BETTER PLAN' right. The chain now reads: NAMED OUTCOME -> ALIGNED PLAN -> STUDENT TRANSFER. The B06 CRIMSON chips are now handled by the NAMED OUTCOME block.
production_viz.note: close the loop — show the fix, not just the problem

beats.narration_text: The artifact and the learning are not the same thing. Claude can make one fast. Only you can ask whether it serves the other.

card.copy: Artifact quality does not predict learning.
card.sub: Name the outcome first. AI IN EDUCATION

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] endcard — artifact quality vs. learning
